title: A Milestone in Formalization: The Sphere Packing Problem in Dimension 8

thm:1.summary: Viazovska's theorem, formalized in Lean: no sphere packing in $\mathbb{R}^8$ has density greater than the $E_8$ packing, with $\Delta_8 = \Delta(\mathcal{P}(\Lambda_8)) = \pi^4/384$. The headline result formally verified in this paper.

thm:2.summary: Cohn–Elkies linear-programming bound (2003), restated as formalized in Lean: a Schwartz $f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}$ with $f$ not identically zero, $f(x)\le 0$ for $\|x\|\ge 1$, and $\widehat{f}(x)\ge 0$ everywhere yields $\Delta_d \le f(0)/\widehat{f}(0)$ — the framework Viazovska's proof plugs into.

edges.note: Viazovska's $E_8$ theorem is derived from the Cohn–Elkies LP bound (Theorem 2) applied to the magic function.

formalized_theorems.description: The two theorems whose Lean formalization this paper reports: Viazovska's $E_8$ density bound and the underlying Cohn–Elkies LP bound. The paper's mathematical content is intentionally a restatement; its novelty is the *formalization* of Viazovska's proof in Lean 4, the construction of the magic function in Lean, the modular-forms infrastructure built, and the metaprogramming / autoformalization tooling around it.

Viazovska2016.title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 8 (Viazovska)

CohnElkies.title: New upper bounds on sphere packings I (Cohn–Elkies)
